headline: Prime Video Warns Fans: Set Stalking Threatens Production Magic
lede: Amazon's streaming division draws a hard line against fan intrusions on the set of teen drama 'The Summer I Turned Pretty', citing serious production and safety risks.
body:
Prime Video's social media team fired a direct warning shot to overeager fans this week, publicly calling out disruptive on-set behavior that threatens the delicate ecosystem of television production.

The official social media account for the popular teen drama series made it crystal clear: location sharing and unauthorized set visits are categorically unacceptable. Fan enthusiasm, while appreciated, cannot compromise the fundamental safety and logistical integrity of a working film set.

Production challenges like unauthorized access create significant risks beyond mere inconvenience. Crew members require controlled environments to execute complex shooting schedules. Uninvited visitors can introduce insurance liabilities, disrupt carefully planned shooting sequences, and potentially endanger talent and technical staff.

The incident highlights an emerging tension between digital fan cultures and professional production standards. Social media platforms have empowered audiences to feel intimately connected to their favorite shows, sometimes blurring critical boundaries between appreciation and invasion.

Streaming platforms increasingly must balance fan engagement with protective protocols. Prime Video's direct public communication suggests a new strategy of transparency and firm boundary-setting with increasingly sophisticated fan communities.
key_facts: Prime Video publicly warned fans about set disruptions
Unauthorized set visits create safety and logistical risks
Teen drama production requires controlled environment
